body: This change applies to the following licences and authorization holders: Federally licensed cannabis producers (federal licence holders) can now promote their cannabis by providing cannabis store licensees product samples for a nominal fee. CRS and PRS licensees and their employees can accept samples from a federal licence holder but are not allowed to supply cannabis samples to other cannabis licensees. There is no prescribed maximum size for cannabis samples. However, cannabis samples are expected to be of reasonable size and quantity and should have little retail value. A sample shall not be used for any type of inducement. These changes to the licence terms and conditions are reflected in the updated: This change to the licence terms and conditions is in response to industry feedback and is a first step in helping federal licence holders to promote cannabis with samples while meeting legislative requirements. To further support the cannabis industry, the LCRB continues to explore future regulatory solutions that will allow: CRS and PRS licensees must keep records relating to cannabis samples that contain the following information (handbook section 3.4.17): a) the unique excise tax identifier from the original packaging of the cannabis sample; b) the date the licensee received the cannabis sample; c) the federal licence holder and name of the representative that provided the cannabis sample; d) the nominal purchase amount that the licensee paid for the cannabis sample; and

minutes_briefs.title: Cannabis producers can now sell samples to retailers for a nominal fee
minutes_briefs.summary: Federally licensed cannabis producers can now promote their products by providing samples to cannabis retail store (CRS) and producer retail store (PRS) licensees for a nominal fee. CRS and PRS licensees and their employees can accept these samples but cannot supply them to other licensees. There is no set maximum size for samples, but they must be reasonable and have little retail value. Samples cannot be used as an inducement. Licensees must keep records of each sample, including the excise tax identifier, date received, supplier details, and amount paid.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Update your record-keeping procedures to track cannabis samples as required by the LCRB handbook section 3.4.17.
